texto: In order to fly you have to create space in the open air so that your wings can really spread out. It’s like a parachute. They only work from a high altitude. To fly you have to begin taking risks. If you don’t want to, maybe the best thing is just to give up, and keep walking forever.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jorge Bucay: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “In order to fly you have to create space in the open air so that your wings can really spread out…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, inspiration, inspiracional pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e trabalho.

frase_original: In order to fly you have to create space in the open air so that your wings can really spread out. It’s like a parachute. They only work from a high altitude. To fly you have to begin taking risks. If you don’t want to, maybe the best thing is just to give up, and keep walking forever.

texto: If someone has children, the first thing they want is for them to be happy, and then become someone in life and all that. But the educational system, I mean always, not just now, creates competitive, successful people, and does not educate them to be happy. The problem is that success gives money, not happiness. The eternal problem.
explicacao: Citação de Jorge Bucay sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “If someone has children, the first thing they want is for them to be happy, and then become someo…” articula felicidade, vida, success em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como success e children.
